sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Rapport Therapeutics CDO Yeleswaram Krishnaswamy sells $808,844 stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Rapport Therapeutics' Chief Development Officer, Yeleswaram Krishnaswamy, sold 20,225 shares of the company's common stock for over $808,000 on April 17, 2026, through a pre-arranged trading plan. Despite the sale, the company's stock has seen a significant annual increase, though it is currently considered overvalued according to InvestingPro. Meanwhile, Rapport Therapeutics recently announced positive Phase 2a trial results for its epilepsy drug RAP-219, leading to accelerated Phase 3 trials and reiterated "Buy" ratings from several analysts.

feed.title: Simplify Hedged Equity ETF Offers Protection Against Volatile Markets
feed.summary: The Simplify Hedged Equity ETF (HEQT) aims to protect investors during volatile markets by maintaining exposure to the S&P 500 while employing a put spread collar options strategy. This strategy involves purchasing and selling put options along with a covered call to buffer against losses, although it caps potential gains and may come with higher management fees. The fund manages timing risk by layering these collars across different monthly expiration dates, offering a tactical option for risk management rather than maximizing total returns.

feed.title: Latest MPV News - Barings Participation Investors Reports Prel...
feed.summary: This page aggregates news and insights for MassMutual Participation Investors (MPV), a closed-end management investment company advised by Barings LLC. It details quarterly financial results, net investment income, NAV changes, and dividend declarations, including a special dividend in Q4 2024. The Trust focuses on 1st lien senior secured middle-market investments and public fixed income, with regular updates on new investments, add-ons, and realizations.

feed.title: KKR Real Estate: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Inc. (KREF) reported a loss of $56.1 million in its first quarter, translating to a loss of 96 cents per share. After adjusting for non-recurring costs and stock option expenses, the loss was 6 cents per share. The company generated $95.9 million in revenue, with an adjusted revenue of $26.2 million for the period.

feed.title: Mkdwell Tech issues 709,740 ordinary shares under 2026 equity plan
feed.summary: Mkdwell Tech Inc. has announced the issuance of 709,740 new ordinary shares to four individuals under its 2026 Equity Incentive Plan. These shares were registered with the SEC on March 31, 2026. Following this issuance, the company now has 4,258,442 ordinary shares and 274,366 class A preferred shares outstanding, with each preferred share carrying 100 votes.

feed.title: ServiceNow, Inc. 2026 Q1 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation (NYSE:NOW)
feed.summary: ServiceNow, Inc. published its Q1 2026 earnings call presentation. This article provides access to the slide deck presented during the earnings call, allowing investors and interested parties to review the company's financial results and strategic updates. The content specifically mentions the company's NYSE ticker NOW and the Canadian stock symbol NOWS:CA.

feed.title: Air Force Adds $207.9M to Northrop Grumman Contract for Global Logistics Support
feed.summary: The Air Force has awarded Northrop Grumman Systems Corp. a $207.9 million modification to an existing contract for an additional year of global contractor logistics support services, increasing the total contract value to $596 million. This modification involves Foreign Military Sales to the Republic of Korea, Japan, and NATO. The article also details several other significant defense contracts awarded to various companies across the Navy, Defense Logistics Agency, Air Force, and Army.

feed.title: Pfizer Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.43 a Share, Payable June 12 to Shareholders of Record May 8
feed.summary: Pfizer Inc. (NYSE: PFE) has announced that it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.43 per share. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on June 12 to shareholders of record as of May 8.

feed.title: OGIG Surges 8.4% on Oracle, DoorDash Gains
feed.summary: The ALPS O’Shares Global Internet Giants ETF (OGIG) experienced an 8.4% surge last week, driven by significant gains in Oracle and DoorDash. Oracle's rise was attributed to its AI infrastructure power generation deal, while DoorDash benefited from its advancements in autonomous delivery technology. Technology stocks constituted nearly half of the fund's holdings and were key contributors to its overall robust performance.

feed.title: Ford Recalling 140K Rangers Due to Wiring Issue that Could Cause a Fire
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. is recalling 140,201 Ranger midsize pickups from the 2024 to 2026 model years due to a wiring issue in the A-pillar that could cause an electrical short and potentially lead to a fire. The problem involves sunvisor or headliner wiring harnesses, and owners might notice flickering lights in the sunvisor mirror as a precursor. Ford will notify owners by April 27, and dealers will repair the issue at no cost, with the company currently unaware of any related accidents, injuries, or deaths.

feed.title: DVN Stock Price Prediction 2025-2026 | Devon Energy Corporation Forecast | 24/7 Wall St.
feed.summary: 24/7 Wall St. has issued a "HOLD" rating for Devon Energy Corporation (DVN), setting a 12-month target price of $49.13, indicating a 4.26% upside from its current price of $47.12. The analysis includes an optimistic scenario of $56.01 and a conservative scenario of $42.21, with long-term projections extending to 2031 and a 2030 average price target of $56.54. Analyst sentiment is predominantly bullish, with 21 "Buy" and 6 "Hold" ratings from 27 analysts.

feed.title: Packaging Corp of America tops profit estimates as box demand improves (PKG:NYSE)
feed.summary: Packaging Corporation of America (PKG) exceeded profit expectations for the recent quarter, driven by stronger demand and an improved product mix, particularly in corrugated shipments. Although revenue slightly missed analyst targets, the company's shares rose in extended trading. PKG anticipates continued strong demand and higher product prices for the upcoming quarter, contributing to improved adjusted earnings.

feed.title: Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG) Announces Earnings Results
feed.summary: Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG) reported Q1 earnings of $2.40 per share, surpassing analyst estimates of $2.17. Despite the beat, shares dipped 2.9% to $204.48, and the company announced a quarterly dividend of $1.25 per share. Analysts generally maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $229.00.

feed.title: Microchip Technology jumps as semiconductor rally extends amid AI optimism and earnings-season positioning
feed.summary: Microchip Technology (MCHP) stock rose 7.4% due to a broad semiconductor rally driven by AI optimism and positioning ahead of tech earnings. The increase reflects market sentiment on AI-driven demand and improved macro expectations, rather than a specific company-focused announcement. Insider selling and institutional investment shifts are also noted, alongside recent analyst ratings and price targets.

feed.title: HII's Distributed Shipbuilding: Sourcing from 25 Facilities Across 11 States - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII) is adopting a distributed shipbuilding model to address U.S. Navy production delays, sourcing components from 25 facilities in 11 states, supporting 1,000 jobs. This strategy aims to boost production by overcoming labor and physical capacity limitations at their main shipyards. HII is outsourcing structural production and expanding internal capacity, with key partners manufacturing components for various naval vessels.

feed.title: Tim Cook Regrets Maps Flub, Sees Apple Watch as His Proudest Work
feed.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook recently stated that the 2012 launch of Apple Maps was his "first really big mistake" as the company's leader due to its significant issues with directions and accuracy. Despite this, he considers the Apple Watch to be his proudest achievement during his tenure. Cook plans to step down from his role in a few months.

feed.title: Broadcom just became the sixth company in U.S. history to achieve this feat
feed.summary: Broadcom's market capitalization has surpassed $2 trillion, making it the sixth U.S. company to reach this milestone. This achievement follows a strong day for chip stocks and Google's announcement of new TPUs. The other companies to have achieved this feat are Nvidia, Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, and Apple.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) Director Sells $254,280.00 in Stock
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) Director James Thompson sold 1,000 shares of company stock for $254,280 on April 21st, reducing his stake by 4.86%. This sale follows the company's strong first-quarter earnings, where it surpassed analyst EPS and revenue estimates, and increased its quarterly dividend. Despite the insider sale, JBHT maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with shares trading near a 52-week high.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt Transport Services (NASDAQ:JBHT) COO Nicholas Hobbs Sells 1,272 Shares
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services COO Nicholas Hobbs sold 1,272 shares of the company's stock for $318,954, reducing his position by 1.37%. The sale occurred after the company reported strong Q1 earnings, beating analyst estimates, and increased its quarterly dividend. Analysts currently rate J.B. Hunt as a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $227.04.

feed.title: Veteran-Owned Expedia Cruises Opens New Wilmington Location at Branmar Plaza, Expanding Personalized Travel Services
feed.summary: Edward Rosenberg, a Navy veteran, and Jenny Eisenberg are opening their second Expedia Cruises location in Delaware at Branmar Plaza in Wilmington. The new franchise will offer personalized travel planning services and will host a grand opening event on Thursday, May 7, featuring prizes, travel partners, and community dignitaries. This expansion reflects their commitment to providing expert travel guidance, building on the success of their first storefront and their A+ Better Business Bureau rating.

feed.title: Assessing Dominion Energy (D) Valuation After Recent Share Price Swings And Offshore Wind Ambitions
feed.summary: Dominion Energy (D) has seen mixed short-term share price returns but a positive 19.7% over the past year, leading to questions about its current valuation. The company's valuation narrative, which includes large-scale offshore wind investments, suggests it is modestly undervalued with a fair value of $66.35 per share compared to a recent close of $61.09. However, potential risks like rising project costs and regulatory limitations could impact future returns.

feed.title: Matador Resources Declares Quarterly Dividend, Signals Disciplined Returns
feed.summary: Matador Resources (MTDR) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.375 per share, payable June 5, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 8, 2026. This dividend, declared under a policy adopted in October 2025, emphasizes that future dividends will be at the board's discretion, linked to the company's operating performance, financial health, capital requirements, and broader business conditions. Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, rates MTDR as an Outperform, citing strong financial performance, valuation, and disciplined operations.

feed.title: Arista Networks Poised to Benefit from Google’s Virgo. The Stock Set a Record.
feed.summary: Arista Networks is expected to gain significantly from Google's new Virgo Network, which will serve as the artificial intelligence hypercomputer's networking system. Evercore ISI analysts highlighted this opportunity, contributing to Arista Networks' stock setting a new record. The article details how Arista's technology aligns with Google's AI infrastructure development.

feed.title: Cintas (CTAS) director sells 4,666 shares after exercising options
feed.summary: Cintas Corporation director Ronald W. Tysoe reported exercising options for 5,500 shares, followed by a tax withholding of 834 shares and an open-market sale of 4,666 shares at an approximate weighted average price of $178.87 per share. After these transactions, Tysoe directly holds 22,448 Cintas common shares. All figures provided reflect the company's four-for-one stock split completed on September 4, 2024.

feed.title: International Paper (IP) slides 5.6% as investors price in pre-earnings downside risk and cost/pricing headwinds
feed.summary: International Paper (IP) stock fell 5.6% due to investor cautiousness ahead of its earnings report, driven by expected cost headwinds and less favorable pricing in containerboard. A major bank placed IP on a negative catalyst watch, forecasting EBITDA below consensus and company targets. Despite these concerns, insider trading shows more purchases than sales, and institutional investors have mixed positions.

feed.title: Adobe EVP & CFO Daniel Durn sells $331,354 in stock
feed.summary: Adobe's EVP & CFO, Daniel Durn, sold 1,336 shares of company stock for approximately $331,354 on April 20, 2026, holding 42,832.787 shares afterward. This sale occurred while Adobe's stock has declined about 30% in the last six months, though InvestingPro analysis suggests it is undervalued with strong gross profit margins. The company recently announced a $25 billion stock buyback program and continues to focus on AI innovation to address investor concerns.

feed.title: International Business Machines (NYSE:IBM) Releases Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $0.10 EPS
feed.summary: International Business Machines (IBM) reported its quarterly earnings, exceeding analyst estimates with an EPS of $1.91 against a consensus of $1.81, and revenues of $15.92 billion compared to $15.60 billion. Despite the strong quarterly performance and a 2.7% dividend yield, IBM shares traded down about 1.5% to $251.72 on higher than average volume. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $298.81 for the stock.

feed.title: Citadel entities report near-10% stake in Momentus Inc. (MNTS)
feed.summary: Citadel Advisors and affiliated entities have filed a Schedule 13G, disclosing a beneficial ownership of 9.97% in Momentus Inc.'s Class A common stock, totaling 634,285 shares for Citadel Advisors LLC, Citadel Advisors Holdings LP, and Citadel GP LLC. Kenneth Griffin is reported to beneficially own 635,792 shares, representing 9.99% of the outstanding shares. The filing indicates shared voting and dispositive power for these holdings and specifies that the percentages are based on 6,364,291 shares outstanding, including warrants convertible into 184,285 shares subject to a 9.99% beneficial ownership limit.

feed.title: Lululemon names former Nike executive O’Neill its next CEO
feed.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed Heidi O’Neill, former Nike Inc. president of consumer, product and brand, as its new chief executive officer, effective September 8. O'Neill takes the helm during a challenging period for Lululemon, marked by slowing growth, product issues, and investor concerns, including a significant stake by Elliott Investment Management and public criticism from founder Chip Wilson. Her appointment comes after previous CEO Calvin McDonald's departure in January.

feed.title: Analog Devices jumps 6.6% as credit upgrade and chip-sector momentum lift sentiment
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) stock rose 6.6% due to an S&P Global Ratings upgrade to "A" with a stable outlook, driven by resilient markets and product leadership. The company's Q1 2026 results and Q2 guidance were positive, contributing to broader semiconductor sector gains fueled by AI optimism. Insider selling has significantly outpaced buying, and institutional investors show mixed activity, while several analysts have issued "Buy" or "Outperform" ratings with a median price target of $400.0.

feed.title: CSX shares jump 6% as efficiency gains drive bottom-line beat in Q1
feed.summary: CSX Corp. exceeded Q1 analyst expectations with adjusted earnings of $0.43 per share, despite a slight revenue miss at $3.48 billion. The railroad operator's shares rose 6.9% as investors reacted positively to improved profitability driven by efficiency gains and strong expense management. Operating income increased significantly to $1.25 billion, with net earnings reaching $807 million, primarily due to a 3% increase in total volume and higher pricing.

feed.title: Urgent.ly (ULY) updates solicitation filing as $5.50-per-share offer proceeds
feed.summary: Urgent.ly Inc. (ULY) has updated its Schedule 14D-9 solicitation filing in response to the $5.50 per share cash tender offer from Medford Hawk, Inc., a subsidiary of Agero, Inc. The amendment primarily adds a text message from the information agent, D.F. King, to shareholders as an exhibit. This procedural filing clarifies communications regarding the existing offer without altering its $5.50 per share price or other terms.

feed.title: Delta Beats Pilots’ Military Service Bias Claims at 11th Circuit
feed.summary: The Eleventh Circuit Court of Appeals ruled in favor of Delta Air Lines, affirming that the company had legitimate, non-discriminatory reasons for the departure of two former pilots, James Doyle and Adam McLean. The pilots had claimed they were forced out due to their military service. However, the court found that Delta's investigations into their alleged abuse of sick leave and military leave policies provided sufficient grounds for their resignations to avoid termination.

feed.title: Norwegian (NCLH) Q1 2025 Earnings Transcript
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) reported strong Q1 2025 results, exceeding guidance on net yield and adjusted EBITDA, driven by healthy per diem growth and cost discipline. Despite some recent choppiness in Q3 European bookings, the company maintained its full-year adjusted EBITDA and EPS guidance, emphasizing disciplined pricing and accelerated cost-saving initiatives. NCLH highlighted strategic fleet optimization, including new ship deliveries and vessel charters, alongside enhancements to itineraries and digital platforms to drive long-term value and meet 2026 financial targets.

feed.title: Does Q3 EPS Expectations Pressure Parker-Hannifin’s Earnings Consistency Narrative and Long-Term Outlook (PH)?
feed.summary: Parker-Hannifin recently reported its Q3 results, with diluted EPS expected to be $7.81, a 12.5% increase. The company has consistently beaten EPS forecasts, raising questions about whether this trend will continue and how double-digit EPS growth expectations impact its long-term outlook. This performance is set against existing guidance of $30.40 to $31.00 adjusted EPS for the full year, with forecasts suggesting $24.5 billion revenue and $4.5 billion earnings by 2029.

feed.title: United Rentals Posts Record Q1 Results, Raises 2026 Outlook
feed.summary: United Rentals (URI) reported record first-quarter 2026 results, with total revenue of $3.985 billion and significant increases in rental revenue, net income, EPS, and adjusted EBITDA. The company also raised its full-year 2026 guidance for revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and capital expenditures, driven by confidence in large-project demand and its ability to maintain industry leadership. A Buy rating with a $1071.00 price target was issued by an analyst, and TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates URI as "Outperform" due to strong fundamentals and a positive earnings outlook.

feed.title: Packaging Corp of America beats Q1 profit estimates as demand improves
feed.summary: Packaging Corp of America (PKG) has exceeded its first-quarter profit estimates, indicating an improvement in demand. The brief report from Refinitiv highlights this positive financial performance for the company.

feed.title: Walmart Inc. (WMT) Diversifies Beyond Retail Into Maintenance Services
feed.summary: Walmart Inc. (WMT) has announced its expansion beyond traditional retail by launching a new business unit called Upstream Facility Services. This unit will offer maintenance services, including HVAC, refrigeration, general maintenance, electrical, and plumbing, to other businesses across the country. The move leverages Walmart's existing internal facility maintenance operations to provide urgent repairs, preventive, and predictive services to external clients, supported by technology for real-time visibility.

feed.title: Lululemon names former Nike exec Heidi O’Neill as next CEO
feed.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed Heidi O'Neill, a former Nike executive, as its new chief executive officer, effective September 8th. O'Neill will also join the company's board of directors. This change comes after shareholder criticism regarding Lululemon's share price and sales performance, following the previous CEO's resignation in December.

feed.title: Lululemon names former Nike exec Heidi O’Neill as next CEO
feed.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed former Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as its next chief executive officer, effective September 8. This decision comes after months of criticism regarding the company's management and performance, including calls from estranged founder Chip Wilson for new board members. O’Neill, credited with helping Nike grow significantly during her 25-year tenure, will also join Lululemon's board.

feed.title: MEDIROM Calls May 29 Shareholder Meeting to Approve FY2025 Results and Expand Board
feed.summary: MEDIROM Healthcare Technologies (MRM) will hold a shareholder meeting on May 29, 2026, to approve its unaudited non-consolidated FY2025 financial statements and to re-elect four directors while adding one new member to its board. This move aims to strengthen the company's governance structure, balancing Japanese legal requirements with U.S. market reporting obligations. Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates MRM as Neutral, citing strong revenue growth but concerns about profitability, liquidity, and a bearish technical trend, offset by an undervalued P/E ratio.

feed.title: Western Gateway Pipeline Advances as Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan Secure Shipper Commitments
feed.summary: Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan have secured sufficient long-term shipping commitments to proceed with the Western Gateway Pipeline project. This pipeline aims to transport refined fuels from Midwest and Gulf Coast refineries to Arizona and California, with a link to Las Vegas, potentially easing regional fuel supply constraints. However, the project may encounter continued political and regulatory strictness in California due to the state's environmental policies on fossil fuels.

feed.title: Microsoft to retire Outlook Lite: What users need to do next
feed.summary: Microsoft is discontinuing its Outlook Lite app on May 25, 2026, and urges users to transition to the more robust Outlook Mobile app for continued email access. Outlook Lite, launched in 2022 and surpassing 10 million downloads, was a streamlined version designed for lower-powered Android devices. This move is part of Microsoft's strategy to consolidate its email services.

feed.title: Packaging Corp.: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Packaging Corp. of America (PKG) reported first-quarter net income of $170.9 million, or $1.91 per share, with adjusted earnings of $2.40 per share, surpassing analyst expectations. Despite beating profit forecasts, the company's revenue of $2.37 billion for the period fell short of Wall Street estimates. This snapshot highlights the company's financial performance for the quarter, noting a strong earnings per share but a slight miss on revenue.

feed.title: Meritage Homes Corp reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
feed.summary: Meritage Homes Corp has released its earnings summary for the quarter that ended on March 31. The report, provided by Refinitiv and distributed by Reuters, details the company's financial performance for the specified period.

feed.title: Meritage: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Meritage Homes Corp. reported first-quarter earnings of $55.3 million, or 82 cents per share, falling short of Wall Street expectations. Adjusted earnings were 86 cents per share, while analysts had predicted $1.01 per share. The homebuilder's revenue of $1.12 billion also missed forecasts.

feed.title: K Wave Media Restructures Insider Holdings With $2 Million Share Purchase Through Subsidiary
feed.summary: K Wave Media (KWM) adjusted an insider share contribution plan from October 2025 to mitigate tax issues and realign ownership. Its subsidiary, K Enter Holdings Inc., purchased 4.77 million shares for $2 million between December 19-23, 2025, with 10% paid upfront and the remainder due by June 30, 2026. This transaction shifts a significant block of insider-owned stock into corporate hands, affecting the company's capital management and effective stock float.

feed.title: Directors Plant, Tyagarajan remain on Jabil (NYSE: JBL) board
feed.summary: Jabil Inc. announced that directors John Plant and Tiger Tyagarajan will remain on its board despite failing to receive a majority of votes at the 2026 annual stockholders meeting. Following conditional resignations, the Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee and the Board reviewed their qualifications and contributions, ultimately deciding their continued service is in the best interests of the company and its stockholders. Both directors will serve until the 2027 annual meeting unless they resign earlier or are removed.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (PKG) Packaging Corporation of America Reports Q1 Revenue $2.37B, vs. FactSet Est of $2.43B
feed.summary: Packaging Corporation of America (PKG) reported Q1 revenue of $2.37 billion, falling short of FactSet's estimated $2.43 billion. This financial update indicates that the company's revenue performance for the quarter did not meet analyst expectations. The news was published on MarketScreener.

feed.title: NIKE (NKE) CEO boosts stake with April open-market share buy
feed.summary: NIKE, Inc.'s President & CEO, Elliott Hill, acquired 23,660.235 shares of Class B Common Stock on April 13, 2026, at a weighted average price of $42.27 per share. This open-market purchase increased his direct holdings to 265,247.235 NIKE Class B shares. The amended Form 4/A was filed to clarify the weighted average price resulting from multiple trades.
